Overall, first East convention awesome, somewhat mixed bag.
won't write about negatives much, them way.
Each room Hynes Convention Center undersized purpose.
main event room only held about 5,000 ~45,000 present each day.
opening ceremonies (Wil Wheaton keynote first Mike Jerry Q&A) filled still waited over hour line turned away door.
Panel rooms were small that attendance typically required 45-60 minutes queue-ing.
Tabletop gaming split into eight rooms across floors!
Coordination needs improvement.
enforcers need team leaders with walkie-talkies.
halls needed more signage about where going what's nearby.
Panels needed better organization, like track system Dragon*Con uses.
should open full three days.
Doors open until Friday things wrapped around Sunday.
would like doors open 10am Friday.
There just wasn't enough time everything.
Heck, didn't fully explore Expo Hall even console computer game rooms.
elected skip panels altogether lieu better time management.
only briefly Rock Band room, would have liked play song with friends.
enough dragging East down.
They moving bigger convention center next year will have feedback they need from this year.
brought entire suitcase games East.
kidding.

prove much we've played game, took second third, respectively.

blast even medal signed Wheaton!

God, concerts.
First off, sure wasn't getting into either night concerts based size event room.
However, insanely lucky able walk into room both nights, just time acts wanted play.
Both nights, technical difficulty other delays turned into brilliant timing
Friday, Protomen Anamanaguchi.
heard Protomen's self-titled album once twice figured they were worth checking out.
Holy hell, they were.
completely blown away Protomen's showmanship.
Make-up, costumes, atmosphere, choreography... total mind blowage.
I've never seen much fist-pumping life.
favorite part their chip tune music, stage their guitars were overpowering.
front very cheerful easy tell happy there were.
They played some tunes it's clear they have bright future.
Absolutely check their cheery chip tune rock haven't yet.
Saturday, showed just time Paul Storm stage with Wheaton this magical sketch- never seen Paul Storm Jonathan Coulton live before.
definitely treat.
Paul Storm superb musical comedians write catchy folk music.
Their ability engage audience inspiring entertaining.
I've been listening JoCo songs years now, seeing live also treat.
didn't anything super special outside live performance work.
Distract-a-tron cool.
Like said before, great time, blast, will certainly going future PAXs, this some kinks work out, which have full faith Penny Arcade resolve.
